title: World Perception Challenge

introduction.subtitle: How realistic is your view of the world? 12 questions, real open data, no moralizing.
introduction.description:
This is not a trivia contest. It tests your intuition about global trends in health, economics, demographics, and climate — against open data from the World Bank, Our World in Data, and the WHO. Your wrong answers are informative: they reveal where your worldview is pessimistic, optimistic, or selectively distorted.

Each question has one answer closest to the data and two plausible distractors. Pick the one you believe is closest.

Use the result as language for reflection and conversation, not as a permanent label. The terms are meant to make patterns easier to notice, compare, and discuss.
introduction.privacyNote: Your answers stay in this browser. The result belongs to you. Sharing is your choice.
introduction.disclaimer: Estimates reflect latest available statistics as of 2023-2024. Figures are approximate; sources are cited per question. This is an intuition test, not a knowledge exam.

questions.text: What percentage of the world's one-year-old children are vaccinated against measles?

conceptHelp.title: Health Perception: What percentage of the world's one-year-old children are vaccinated against measles
conceptHelp.summary: Health Perception means reading global health progress and limits from comparable data rather than memorable news. This question asks for the measured pattern behind "What percentage of the world's one-year-old children are vaccinated against measles?".
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Many wrong answers feel plausible because public attention overweights dramatic events and underweights slow improvement or scale. The learning value is in calibrating intuition to data.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Pause before guessing from headlines. Compare the options against broad base rates, long-term direction, and the scale implied by the numbers.

questions.text: How has the global under-five mortality rate (per 1,000 live births) changed since 1990?
questions.source: UN IGME / Our World in Data

conceptHelp.title: Health Perception: How has the global under-five mortality rate (per 1,000 live births) changed since 1990
conceptHelp.summary: Health Perception means reading global health progress and limits from comparable data rather than memorable news. This question asks for the measured pattern behind "How has the global under-five mortality rate (per 1,000 live births) changed since 1990?".
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Many wrong answers feel plausible because public attention overweights dramatic events and underweights slow improvement or scale. The learning value is in calibrating intuition to data.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Pause before guessing from headlines. Compare the options against broad base rates, long-term direction, and the scale implied by the numbers.
conceptHelp.example: The strongest answer is: "Roughly halved, from 93 to about 37"

questions.text: How has the share of the world's population living in extreme poverty changed since 1990?
questions.source: World Bank Poverty and Inequality Platform

options.text: Decreased by more than half (from ~38% to ~9%)

conceptHelp.title: Economic Perception: How has the share of the world's population living in extreme poverty changed since 1990
conceptHelp.summary: Economic Perception means reading poverty, living standards, literacy, and infrastructure from measured long-term trends. This question asks for the measured pattern behind "How has the share of the world's population living in extreme poverty changed since 1990?".
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Many wrong answers feel plausible because public attention overweights dramatic events and underweights slow improvement or scale. The learning value is in calibrating intuition to data.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Pause before guessing from headlines. Compare the options against broad base rates, long-term direction, and the scale implied by the numbers.
conceptHelp.example: The strongest answer is: "Decreased by more than half (from ~38% to ~9%)"

questions.text: How have global CO2 emissions from fossil fuels changed since 2000?
questions.source: Global Carbon Project / Our World in Data

conceptHelp.title: Climate Perception: How have global CO2 emissions from fossil fuels changed since 2000
conceptHelp.summary: Climate Perception means reading climate and energy facts from data about emissions, electricity mix, and technology costs. This question asks for the measured pattern behind "How have global CO2 emissions from fossil fuels changed since 2000?".
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Many wrong answers feel plausible because public attention overweights dramatic events and underweights slow improvement or scale. The learning value is in calibrating intuition to data.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Pause before guessing from headlines. Compare the options against broad base rates, long-term direction, and the scale implied by the numbers.
conceptHelp.example: The strongest answer is: "Increased by about 30%"

attribution.text: Data from World Bank Open Data (CC BY 4.0), Our World in Data (CC BY), and WHO. Estimates reflect latest available statistics as of 2023-2024.

questionStageTitle: Learn the concept, then answer from evidence
questionStageDescription: Use the concept button when a term is unfamiliar. Prefer observable evidence over aspiration or ideal self-description.
